row.sectionName: Parasternal Long Axis
row.column:
Aortic Valve (AV)
Right Coronary Cusp (RCC)
Non coronary cusp (NCC) Descending Aorta (DA)
Inter-ventricular Septum (IVS)
Left Atrium (LA)
Left Ventricle (LV)
Anteroseptal (IVS)
Inferolateral (IL) Mitral Valve (MV)
Anterior Mitral Valve Leaflet (AMVL)
Posterior Mitral Valve Leaflet (PMVL) Right Ventricular Outflow Tract (RVOT)

column.li: with the index marker pointing towards the right shoulder (11 o'clock)
10-16cm to see the descending aorta in SAX
increase to 20cm to assess the left pleural space

h.b: Adjust the TTE probe position to:

column.li: place the MV and AV in the center of the image (tilt probe away from sternum)
make the AV cusps appear symmetric (rotate probe)
make the IVS and posterior LV wall appear horizontal (change interspace)
ensure the LV apex is not seen (rotate probe)
move the probe one interspace higher to image the Ascending Aorta in LAX
LV: size, function and wall thickness
Aortic root: size, dilation and dissection

row.sectionName: Parasternal Long Axis Right Ventricular Inflow View
row.column:
Coronary Sinus (CS)
Inferior Vena Cava (IVC)
Right Atrium (RA) Right Ventricle (RV)
Inferior (I or IVS)
RV free wall or Anterolateral (AL) Superior Vena Cava (SVC)
Tricuspid Valve (TV)
Anterior Tricuspid Valve leaflet (ATVL)
Posterior Tricuspid Valve Leaflet (PTVL)

column.li: modification of the Parasternal LAX view
with the index marker pointing towards the right shoulder (11 o'clock)
tilted inferiorly and medially (towards R hip)

h.b: Adjust the TTE probe position to:

column.li: visualize only the RV and not the LV
center the image (slide probe away from sternum)
make the TV cusps appear symmetric (rotate probe clockwise)
visualize the RV apex (change one interspace lower)

row.sectionName: Parasternal Long Axis Right Ventricular Outflow View
row.column:
Descending Aorta (DA)
Left Atrium (LA)
Left Upper Pulmonary Vein (LUPV)*
Main Pulmonary Artery (MPA)
Pulmonic Valve (PV)
Right Pulmonary Artery (RPA)*
Right Ventricular Outflow Tract (RVOT)
*not often seen in TTE image

column.li: modification of the Parasternal LAX view
with the index marker pointing towards the right shoulder (11 o'clock)

h.b: Adjust the TTE probe position to:

column.li: center the image (slide probe away from sternum)
make the PV cusps appear symmetric (rotate probe clockwise)
visualize the RV outflow tract (change one interspace higher)

row.sectionName: Parasternal Short Axis Aortic Valve and Right Ventricular Outflow Tract Level
row.column:
Aortic Valve (AV)
Right Coronary Cusp (RCC)
Left Coronary Cusp (LCC)
Non-coronary Cusp (NCC) Descending Aorta (DA)
Interatrial Septum (IAS)
Left Atrium (LA)
Left Atrial Appendage (LAA)* Left Pulmonary Artery (LPA)*
Main Pulmonary Artery (MPA)
Pulmonic Valve (PV)
Right Atrium (RA)
Right Ventricular Outflow Tract (RVOT)
Tricuspid Valve (TV)
Anterior Tricuspid Valve Leaflet (ATVL)
Septal Tricuspid Valve leaflet (STVL) *not often seen in TTE image

column.li: modification of the Parasternal LAX view in which the AV is in the center of the screen
rotate the probe 90° clockwise from the right shoulder (marker at 11 o'clock) so the index marker now points towards the left shoulder (2 o'clock)

h.b: Adjust the TTE probe position to:

column.li: center the image (slide probe away from sternum)
make the AV cusps appear symmetric (rotate probe)
visualize the RV outflow tract (change one interspace higher)
Main and Left Pulmonary Artery: size

row.sectionName: Parasternal Short Axis Left Ventricle at Mitral Valve Level
row.column:
Inter-ventricular Septum (IVS)
Left Ventricle (LV): basal segments
Anterior (A)
Anterolateral (AL)
Inferolateral (IL)
Inferior (I)
Inferoseptal (IS)
Anteroseptal (AS) Mitral Valve:
Anterior Mitral Valve Leaflet (AMVL)
Posterior Mitral Valve Leaflet (PMVL) Right Ventricle (RV)

column.li: modification of the Parasternal SAX AV and RVOT Level view
with the index marker pointing towards the left shoulder (2 o'clock)
slightly tilted inferiorly towards LV apex

h.b: Adjust the TTE probe position to:

column.li: place the LV in the center of the image (rotate probe)
make the LV appear round in shape and the RV crescent shaped (change interspace)
visualize both mitral valve leaflets (tilt or slide probe inferior)
LV: size, function and wall thickness

row.sectionName: Parasternal Short Axis Left Ventricle at Mid Level
row.column:
Anterolateral Papillary Muscle (AL)
Inter-ventricular Septum (IVS)
Left Ventricle (LV)
Anterior (A)
Anterolateral (AL)
Inferolateral (IL)
Inferior (I)
Inferoseptal (IS)
Anteroseptal (AS) Posteromedial Papillary Muscle (PM)
Right Ventricle (RV)

column.li: modification of the Parasternal SAX AV and RVOT Level view
with the index marker pointing towards the left shoulder (2 o'clock)
tilt or slide inferiorly towards LV apex

h.b: Adjust the TTE probe position to:

column.li: place the LV in the center of the image (rotate probe)
make the LV appear round in shape and the RV crescent shaped (change one interspace lower)
visualize both papillary muscles attached to the LV wall (change one interspace lower)
not visualize the mitral valve (tilt more inferior or change one interspace lower)
LV: size, function and wall thickness

row.sectionName: Parasternal Short Axis Left Ventricle at Apex Level
row.column:
Left Ventricle (LV): apical segments
Anterior (A)
Lateral (L)
Inferior (I)
Septal (S) Right Ventricle (RV) apex

column.li: modification of the Parasternal SAX AV and RVOT Level view
with the index marker pointing towards the left shoulder (2 o'clock)
tilt or slide inferiorly towards LV apex
10-14cm to see the entire LV and pericardium

h.b: Adjust the TTE probe position to:

column.li: place the LV in the center of the image (slide probe)
make the LV appear round in shape (change one interspace lower)
no papillary muscles should be seen (tilt or slide inferior)
a small portion of the RV apex may be seen

row.sectionName: Parasternal Pulmonary Artery Bifurcation View
row.column:
Ascending Aorta (Ao)
Descending Aorta (DA)
Left Pulmonary Artery (LPA)
Main Pulmonary Artery (MPA)
Pulmonic Valve (PV)
Right Atrium (RA)
Right Pulmonary Artery (RPA)
Right Ventricular Outflow Tract (RVOT)

column.li: modification of the Parasternal SAX AV and RVOT Level view
with the index marker pointing towards the left shoulder (1-2 o'clock)

h.b: Adjust the TTE probe position to:

column.li: center the image (slide probe away from sternum)
make the main pulmonary artery walls appear symmetric (rotate probe)
visualize the RV outflow tract (change one interspace higher)
AV should not be seen only aorta (tilt probe superior)

row.sectionName: Apical Four Chamber
row.column:
Descending Aorta (DA)
Inter-atrial Septum (IAS)
Left Atrium (LA)
Left Lower Pulmonary Vein (LLPV)
Left Ventricle (LV) walls
Antero-lateral (AL)
Interventricular Septum (IVS), infero-septal (IS)
Apex Mitral Valve (MV):
Anterior Mitral Valve Leaflet (AMVL)
Posterior Mitral Valve Leaflet (PMVL) Right Atrium (RA)
Right Upper Pulmonary Vein (RUPV)
Right Ventricle (RV)
Tricuspid Valve (TV)
Septal Triscuspid Valve Leaflet (STVL)
Anterior Tricuspid Valve Leaflet (ATVL)

column.li: in the midclavicular line or at the point of apical pulsation
with the index marker pointing towards the left (3 o'clock)

h.b: Adjust the TTE probe position to:

column.li: ensure all four cardiac chambers are seen
if the atria are not seen, tilt the probe anterior
if the ventricles are too spherical, move probe down one interspace
make the IVS appear vertical and in the center of the image (move probe laterally)
if LV tilted to right (move probe laterally)
if LV tilted to the left (move probe medially)
not visulaize the AV (if AV is seen the probe is too anterior, tilt probe posterior)
not foreshorten the LV apex (slide probe to apex)
LV apex is thinner then the other LV walls and does not contract

row.sectionName: Apical Five Chamber (Elevated Four Chamber view)
row.column:
Aortic Valve (AV)*
Left Coronary Cusp (LCC)
Right Coronary Cusp (RCC) Inter-ventricular Septum (IVS)
Left Atrium (LA)
Left Ventricle (LV)
Left Ventricular Outflow Tract (LVOT)
Mitral Valve (MV)*
Anterior Mitral Valve Leaflet (AMVL)
Posterior Mitral Valve Leaflet (PMVL) Right Atrium (RA)
Right Ventricle (RV)
Tricuspid Valve (TV)*
Septal Triscuspid Valve Leaflet (STVL)
Anterior Tricuspid Valve Leaflet (ATVL) *may be difficult to distinguish cusps/leaflets in TTE image

column.li: modification of the Apical 4 Chamber view
in the midclavicular line or at the point of apical pulsation
with the index marker pointing towards the left (3 o'clock)
tilt anterior to see the aortic valve

h.b: Adjust the TTE probe position to:

column.li: visualize the AV and LVOT (tilt probe anterior)
LVOT alignment (higher interspace and slide probe lateral)
make the IVS appear vertical and in the center of the image
if LV tilted to right (slide probe lateral)
if LV tilted to the left (slide probe medial)
atria and IAS should be incompletely seen

row.sectionName: Apical Two Chamber (Vertical Long Axis view)
row.column:
Coronary Sinus (CS)
Descending Aorta (DA): SAX and LAX
Left Atrium (LA)
Left Atrial Appendage (LAA)* Left Pulmonary Artery (LPA)*
Left Ventricle (LV) walls
Anterior (A)
Inferior (I)
Apex Mitral Valve (MV)*
Anterior Mitral Valve Leaflet (AMVL)
Posterior Mitral Valve Leaflet (PMVL) *not often seen in TTE image

column.li: modification of the Apical 4 Chamber view
in the midclavicular line or at the point of apical pulsation
from the Apical 4 Chamber view, rotate the probe 60-90 degrees counterclockwise
with the index marker now pointing towards the left axilla (1 o'clock)

h.b: Adjust the TTE probe position to:

column.li: ensure the LV, LA and MV are seen
LV apex at the top of the display (slide probe)
Right heart should not be seen (not enough probe rotation)
AV and LVOT should not be seen (too much probe rotation)
Descending aorta can be seen by tilting probe posterior and medial
Descending aorta in LAX rotate probe clockwise

row.sectionName: Apical Long Axis (Three Chamber view)
row.column:
Aortic Valve (AV)
Non-coronary Cusp (NCC)
Right Coronary Cusp (RCC) Ascending Aorta (Ao)
Descending Aorta (DA)
Inter-ventricular Septum (IVS)
Left Atrium (LA)
Left Ventricle (LV) walls
Apex
Infero-lateral (IL)
Antero-septal (AS) Left Ventricular Outflow Tract (LVOT)
Mitral Valve (MV)
Anterior Mitral Valve Leaflet (AMVL)
Posterior Mitral Valve Leaflet (PMVL) Right Ventricle (RV)

column.li: modification of the Apical 2 Chamber view
in the midclavicular line or at the point of apical pulsation
from the Apical 2 Chamber view, rotate 45 degrees counterclockwise and tilt anterior
with the index marker now pointing towards the right shoulder (11 o'clock)

h.b: Adjust the TTE probe position to:

column.li: ensure cardiac chambers are centered (slide probe lateral)
visualize the AV and LVOT (rotate probe)

row.sectionName: Subcostal Four Chamber
row.column:
Inter-atrial Septum (IAS)
Inter-ventricular Septum (IVS)
Left Atrium (LA)
Left Ventricle (LV)
Antero-lateral (AL)
Infero-septal (IS) Liver
Mitral Valve (MV)
Anterior Mitral Valve Leaflet (AMVL)
Posterior Mitral Valve leaflet (PMVL) Right Atrium (RA)
Right Ventricle (RV)
Free (lateral) wall Tricuspid Valve (TV)
Septal Tricuspid Valve Leaflet (STVL)
Anterior Tricuspid Valve Leaflet (ATVL)

column.li: in the subxiphoid region of the abdomen
flat and push down with a slight tilt to the patient's right
with the index marker pointing towards the left (3 o'clock)
16-24cm to image the entire LA and LV

h.b: Adjust the TTE probe position to:

column.li: see entire LV including apex (slide probe)
not visualize the AV (if AV seen then tilt probe posterior or rotate the probe)
lengthen the IVS (rotate probe so marker at 2-3 o'clock)

row.sectionName: Subcostal Aortic Valve and Right Ventricular Outflow Tract
row.column:
Aortic Valve (AV)
Right Coronary Cusp (RCC)
Left Coronary Cusp (LCC)
Non-coronary Cusp (NCC) Inter-atrial Septum (IAS)
Left Atrium (LA)
Main Pulmonary Artery (MPA)
Pulmonic Valve (PV)
Right Atrium (RA)
Right Ventricular Outflow Tract (RVOT)
Tricuspid Valve (TV)
Septal Tricuspid Valve Leaflet (STVL)
Anterior Tricuspid Valve Leaflet (ATVL)

column.li: in the subxiphoid region of the abdomen
flat and push down with a slight tilt to the patient's right
from the subcostal 4C view, rotate the probe the probe 90° counterclockwise
with the index marker now pointing towards the head (12 o'clock)

h.b: Adjust the TTE probe position to:

column.li: visualize the Aortic Valve (tilt probe towards midline)
make the AV cusps appear symmetric (rotate probe)
Right Ventricular Outflow Tract: size, obstruction
Atrial Septal Defect: Sinus Venosus Type

row.sectionName: Subcostal Left Ventricle at Mitral Valve
row.column:
Descending Aorta (DA)*
Inter-ventricular Septum (IVS)
Left Ventricle (LV): basal segments
Anterior (A)
Anterolateral (AL)
Inferolateral (IL)
Inferior (I)
Inferoseptal (IS)
Anteroseptal (AS) Mitral valve:
Anterior Mitral Valve Leaflet (AMVL)
Posterior Mitral Valve Leaflet (PMVL) Right Ventricle (RV)
*may not be seen in TTE image

column.li: in the subxiphoid region of the abdomen
flat and push down with a slight tilt to the patient's right
from the subcostal 4C view, rotate the probe the probe 90° counterclockwise
with the index marker now pointing towards the head (12 o'clock)
turn the probe towards the patient's right (more midline)

h.b: Adjust the TTE probe position to:

column.li: place the LV in the center of the image (slide probe)
make the LV appear round in shape (rotate probe)
Similar to Parasternal SAX LV at Mitral Valve Level

row.sectionName: Subcostal Left Ventricle at Mid
row.column:
Interolateral Papillary Muscle (AL)
Descending Aorta (DA)*
Inter-ventricular Septum (IVS)
Left Ventricle (LV)
Anterior (A)
Anterolateral (AL)
Inferolateral (IL)
Inferior (I)
Inferoseptal (IS)
Anteroseptal (AS) Posteromedial Papillary Muscle (PM)
Right Ventricle (RV)
*not often seen in TTE image

column.li: in the subxiphoid region of the abdomen
flat and push down with a slight tilt to the patient's right
from the subcostal 4C view, rotate the probe the probe 90° counterclockwise
with the index marker now pointing towards the head (12 o'clock)

h.b: Adjust the TTE probe position to:

column.li: place the LV in the center of the image (slide probe)
make the LV appear round in shape (rotate probe)
visualize both papillary muscles attached to the LV wall (tilt probe)
Similar to Parasternal SAX LV at Mid Level

row.sectionName: Subcostal Left Ventricle at Apex
row.column:
Left Ventricle (LV)
Anterior (A)
Lateral (L)
Inferior (I)
Septal (S) Right Ventricle (RV)

column.li: in the subxiphoid region of the abdomen
flat and push down with a slight tilt to the patient's right
from the subcostal 4C view, rotate the probe the probe 90° counterclockwise
with the index marker now pointing towards the head (12 o'clock)

h.b: Adjust the TTE probe position to:

column.li: place the LV in the center of the image (slide probe)
make the LV appear round in shape (rotate probe)
no papillary muscles should be seen (tilt probe)
a small portion of the RV apex may be seen
Similar to Parasternal SAX at Apex

row.sectionName: Subcostal Inferior Vena Cava
row.column:
Inferior Vena Cava (IVC)
Liver
Right Atrium (RA)
Hepatic Vein (HV)

column.li: in the subxiphoid region of the abdomen
from the subcostal 4C view , rotate the probe 90° counterclockwise while keeping the RA in view
with the index marker pointing towards the head (12 o'clock)

h.b: Adjust the TTE probe position to:

column.li: view the junction of the IVC and RA (IVC must be shown to enter the RA)

h.b: Differentiate the IVC from the aorta by:

column.li: look for liver surrounding both sides of IVC
no change in aorta size with respiration
change in IVC size with respiration

row.sectionName: Suprasternal Long Axis of Aortic Arch
row.column:
Aorta
Ascending (Ao)
Arch
Descending (DA) Right Brachiocephalic Artery (RBCA)
Left Atrium (LA)
Left Common Carotid Artery (LCCA)
Left Subclavian Artery (LSA)
Left Innominate Vein
Right Pulmonary Artery (RPA)

column.li: with the index marker pointing towards the left supraclavicular notch (1 o'clock)

h.b: Adjust the TTE probe position to:

column.li: view the descending aorta (tilt more anterior)
view the head vessels (tilt more posterior)
open the lumen of the aorta and see arch (rotate probe)

row.sectionName: Right Parasternal Ascending Aorta
row.column:
Ascending Aorta(Ao)
Ascending (ASC)
Arch
Descending (DA) Aortic Valve (AV)
Descending Aorta (DA)*
Left Atrium (LA)
Left Ventricle (LV)*
Mitral Valve (MV)*
Right pulmonary Artery (RPA)
*not often seen in TTE image

column.li: with the index marker pointing towards the left clavicle (1 o'clock)
turn 90° counterclockwise to see the Ascending Aorta in SAX
10-16cm to see the ascending aorta in LAX

h.b: Adjust the TTE probe position to:
